reason: The remediation requires editing the GRUB2 bootloader configuration and regenerating the boot menu. Mistakes can render the system unbootable or lock out console access, which aligns with the policy that bootloader changes are considered too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential boot failure; Loss of console/SSH access if GRUB configuration is corrupted; Inability to modify boot parameters without rescue media
